assertions.title: WebGL Three.js Logo Reveal Replaces LobsterIntro

steps.observed: No matches found — LobsterIntro is not imported anywhere in src/
Line 8: const CinematicIntro = lazy(() => import('@/components/landing/CinematicIntro'))
CinematicIntro.tsx imports Canvas from @react-three/fiber, uses <Canvas> with WebGL shader material, Three.js scene, bloom, and vignette post-processing
WZRD.tech logo rendered with bloom/glow effects on black background, orange ambient particles visible — confirmed WebGL rendering

steps.expected: No caustic overlay, lobster silhouette, ember particles, or thermal text
steps.observed: All LobsterIntro sub-components absent from DOM (causticOverlay: false, lobsterSilhouette: false, emberParticles: false, thermalText: false)

evidence.consoleErrors: Only pre-existing lovable-tagger errors (Cannot read properties of undefined reading 'lov') — no WebGL or intro-related errors
evidence.codeInspection: LobsterIntro.tsx exists but is NOT imported in Landing.tsx or any other file. CinematicIntro.tsx uses Three.js <Canvas> with custom GLSL shaders, LogoPlane, AmbientParticles, Bloom, and Vignette.

assertions.title: Intro Plays Automatically on First Visit

steps.observed: sessionStorage.removeItem('mog-intro-seen') executed successfully

steps.expected: Intro plays automatically covering full viewport
steps.observed: WebGL intro animation played automatically — WZRD.tech logo with bloom effects visible covering viewport (black background). Screenshot captured mid-animation.
sessionStorage.getItem('mog-intro-seen') === 'true' — confirmed auto-set after completion

steps.expected: Fixed overlay with inset-0 and z-[99999]
steps.observed: CinematicIntro renders with className 'fixed inset-0 z-[99999]' — covers full viewport
Intro animation visible with WZRD.tech logo and bloom effects
Screenshot shows intro mid-animation with logo and orange glow
Intro dismissed — clicking triggered handleSkip which sets phase='complete' and calls onComplete()
Landing page fully visible with WZRD Studio headline, Agent-Native badge, feature cards
sessionStorage.getItem('mog-intro-seen') === 'true'
introPresent: false — overlay completely removed from DOM

assertions.title: Intro Does Not Replay in Same Session

steps.observed: Successfully navigated to http://localhost:8080/home
Landing page content shown directly — no intro overlay, no canvas, no animation
introPresent: false, canvasPresent: false, mogIntroSeen: 'true' — intro correctly suppressed
Intro animation started automatically
After 8s wait, intro fully completed and dismissed

steps.expected: Landing page visible, no orphaned elements
steps.observed: Landing page content visible (WZRD Studio headline, feature cards). No intro overlay, no canvas elements, no orphaned DOM elements.
mogIntroSeen: 'true' — auto-set by onComplete callback
isWebGLAvailable() function at lines 10-15 checks for webgl2/webgl/experimental-webgl context. Returns false if unavailable.

steps.action: Inspect fallback behavior when WebGL unavailable
steps.expected: Graceful fallback — skip to landing page
steps.observed: Lines 332-335: if (!webglAvailable) { onComplete(); return; } — immediately completes intro when WebGL unavailable
WebGLErrorBoundary class component catches errors, calls onError → handleWebGLError → onComplete(). Renders fallback (null) on error.

steps.expected: Canvas has fallback for rendering failures
steps.observed: Canvas component has fallback={<div className='w-full h-full bg-black' />}
6000ms safety timeout in useEffect calls onComplete() if phase hasn't reached 'complete'

steps.action: Run unit test: 'skips intro when WebGL is not available'
steps.observed: All 7 CinematicIntro tests passed including 'skips intro when WebGL is not available' — verifies onComplete() called immediately when WebGL unavailable, no canvas rendered

evidence.consoleErrors: No WebGL-related errors in any test scenario
evidence.codeInspection: CinematicIntro.tsx has robust 3-layer fallback: (1) isWebGLAvailable() pre-check skips intro immediately, (2) WebGLErrorBoundary catches runtime rendering errors, (3) 6s safety timeout prevents hangs. Unit test confirms correct behavior.
evidence.unitTests: npx vitest run src/components/landing/__tests__/CinematicIntro.test.tsx — 7/7 passed including WebGL fallback test

frictions.description: Canvas element is extremely transient — created by React Three Fiber during intro animation but removed on phase='complete'. DOM inspection via eval consistently misses it because the intro completes before sequential eval can execute.
frictions.resolution: Verified canvas presence through visual screenshots (clearly showing WebGL-rendered logo with bloom effects) and code inspection confirming <Canvas> from @react-three/fiber is used.

frictions.description: Cannot disable WebGL at runtime for VAL-LAND-006 because JavaScript context resets on page navigation. Monkey-patching HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext before navigation is ineffective.
frictions.resolution: Verified fallback through code inspection and unit tests. The CinematicIntro.test.tsx includes a dedicated 'skips intro when WebGL is not available' test that passes — it mocks WebGL as unavailable and verifies onComplete is called immediately.

frictions.description: Pre-existing console errors from lovable-tagger: 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading lov)' — 16 occurrences. These are unrelated to the intro system.
frictions.resolution: Pre-existing issue noted in AGENTS.md (do NOT remove lovable-related files). Not a regression.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: all 6 passed. The CinematicIntro (Three.js WebGL logo reveal) correctly replaces LobsterIntro, plays automatically on first visit, supports click-to-skip with sessionStorage persistence, does not replay in same session, completes gracefully without skip, and has robust WebGL fallback (verified via code inspection and unit tests).
